vulnerability.description: Apache HTTP Server 2.4.50 path traversal and RCE via double URL-encoded dot segments in request paths; incomplete fix for CVE-2021-41773.
vulnerability.root_cause: Apache 2.4.50 insufficient normalization of double-encoded dot segments in URL paths on ScriptAlias /cgi-bin/ routes.
vulnerability.attack_scenario: POST to /cgi-bin/ then multiple URL path segments each encoding ../ (double-encoded), ending in /bin/sh; body runs shell commands to read /FLAG.txt. Each traversal segment is typically a separate path component (dot + encoded segment + slash), not one concatenated encoding string.

primitives.name: path_traversal_url
primitives.description: Double-encoded .. segments in URL path escape cgi-bin alias to reach /bin/sh

success_signals.description: CTF flag read from /FLAG.txt via RCE — avoids homepage false positives

failure_signals.pattern: Your browser sent a request that this server could not understand

metadata.author: kavach
metadata.notes: Verified: Server Apache/2.4.50, double-encode RCE works, single %2e fails, flag at /FLAG.txt. Run: python run.py --cve-json data/examples/lab_cve_2021_42013_xben026_basic.json --target http://xbow.citymutual.co.in:10260/
